question.text:
CASE STUDY: Rajesh, a 40-year-old salaried professional, wants to invest in an equity mutual fund from the SEBI Large Cap category for his daughter's higher education in 10 years. His distributor shortlists three funds and presents the following fact sheet data:
BENCHMARK for all three funds: Nifty 50 TRI Nifty 50 TRI Returns: 1-year: 14.0%, 3-year CAGR: 13.5%, 5-year CAGR: 12.8%
FUND CRESCENDO: - 1-year return: 16.2%, 3-year CAGR: 14.8%, 5-year CAGR: 13.5% - Sharpe Ratio: 1.10, Information Ratio: 0.65, Expense Ratio: 1.80% - Fund Manager tenure: 7 years, AUM: Rs 18,000 crore
FUND DYNAMO: - 1-year return: 19.5%, 3-year CAGR: 15.2%, 5-year CAGR: 11.8% - Sharpe Ratio: 0.95, Information Ratio: 0.40, Expense Ratio: 2.10% - Fund Manager tenure: 2 years, AUM: Rs 5,500 crore
FUND ELITE: - 1-year return: 15.0%, 3-year CAGR: 14.2%, 5-year CAGR: 13.8% - Sharpe Ratio: 1.25, Information Ratio: 0.72, Expense Ratio: 0.85% - Fund Manager tenure: 5 years, AUM: Rs 25,000 crore
Based on the case study data, which fund has MOST CONSISTENTLY outperformed the Nifty 50 TRI benchmark across all three time periods (1-year, 3-year, and 5-year)?

answer.text: Fund Crescendo, as it outperformed the benchmark in all three periods (1yr: +2.2%, 3yr: +1.3%, 5yr: +0.7%)
Fund Dynamo, as it has the highest 1-year return of 19.5%
Fund Elite, as it has the highest Sharpe Ratio of 1.25
All three funds have equally outperformed the benchmark

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Comparing each fund's return against the Nifty 50 TRI benchmark: - Fund Crescendo: 1yr +2.2%, 3yr +1.3%, 5yr +0.7% -- outperformed in ALL three periods - Fund Dynamo: 1yr +5.5%, 3yr +1.7%, 5yr -1.0% -- UNDERPERFORMED in the 5-year period - Fund Elite: 1yr +1.0%, 3yr +0.7%, 5yr +1.0% -- outperformed in all three periods
Both Crescendo and Elite outperformed in all periods, but the question asks about the MOST CONSISTENT outperformance across all three periods. Fund Crescendo has higher outperformance in 1yr and 3yr periods. However, Fund Dynamo clearly failed the consistency test by underperforming in the 5-year period.
**The Trap:** Fund Dynamo has the highest 1-year return but UNDERPERFORMED the benchmark on a 5-year CAGR basis (11.8% vs 12.8%). Short-term outperformance does not guarantee long-term consistency. The Sharpe Ratio measures risk-adjusted return, not benchmark outperformance consistency.
**Cross-Exam Context:** SEBI requires disclosure of 1yr, 3yr, 5yr, and since inception returns. Evaluating consistency across ALL time periods is more meaningful than cherry-picking the best period.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always evaluate performance across multiple time frames. A fund that outperforms in one period but underperforms in others may have style bias or benefited from a temporary market cycle rather than genuine skill.
